titleStmt.title: SII 10.79: original edition by J. Ramayya Pantulu
No. 79. (A.R. No. 117 of 1917.) ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE MADIGAPALLI OF NŪTAKKI, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT. Ś. 1040.

summary.p: States that Kalugoṭṭumbaḷḷi Māvi-Nāyaka, son of Chāmī-Nāyaka, one of the five hundred mahājanas of Malvāna founded the temple of Kēśavadēva at Nūtakki and gave 25 inupa yeḍlu for a perpetual lamp in the said temple. Describes the Malvāna five hundred as a sect of Brahmins originally coming from Māshapuri on the west of the Gōdāvarī and connects them in some unintelligible way with the killing of Rāvaṇa by Rāma.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī śakavaruṣaṁbu
lu 1040 nēṁṭi Uttarāyaṇaśaṁkrā
ntinimittamuna svasti Anēkaśā
strārtthānvaya vipra vājivaṁśapū
rvvaśrōtriya paṁcaśatabrāhmaṇavē
davēdāṁgōpāṁgāṣṭādaśadharmmasaṁ
yukta laṁkōddēśa lavaṇajalaba
ṁdha rāvaṇavadhāhōma nimitya
vābhāsiddhi gōdāvarīpaścimamā
ṣapuri brāhmaṇa svastidaśarathanu
taviṣṇumūrtti śrīrāghavadatta pa
vanāṁjanēyanurakṣita yajana
yājanādhyayanādhyāpanadānapra
tigrahaṣaṭukarmmaniratu laina
śrīmatu malvāna yēnūrvvuru
mahājanamunaṁdu kalugoṭṭuṁba
ḷli cāmināyakukoḍku māvināya
kuṇḍu nūtekkiṁ gēśavadēvaraṁ bratiṣṭa
sēsi guḍigaṭṭiṁcci Akhaṇḍamunaku nā
caṁdrārkkamu mālenabōyiniko
ḍku cāmasabōyini vasamuna nicci
na yinupa Eḍlu 25 vīniṁ jēkoni ni
tyasāniyaṁbāṭi tavveṇḍu neyi vōyu
cu nukha muṇḍaṁgalavāṇḍu
Digital edition of SII 10.79 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
